# Extração: 14-NDA_for_selling_your_agency

- **Fonte:** 14-NDA_for_selling_your_agency.docx

[YOURCOMPANY], LLC

PRE-ACQUISITION CONFIDENTIALITY, NONCOMPETE and 

NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT



This Agreement made this _____ day of _______________, 20____, by __________________________________ (name of entity receiving confidential information) (hereinafter referred to as the “Company”) with regard to the possible acquisition of the Atlanta, Georgia operations of [YOURCOMPANY], LLC (hereinafter referred to as “[YOURCOMPANY]”).



WITNESSETH:



WHEREAS, the Company desires to obtain certain confidential and proprietary information of [YOURCOMPANY] for the sole purpose of examining said information and determining the feasibility of an acquisition of [YOURCOMPANY]; and



WHEREAS, [YOURCOMPANY] is willing to provide such confidential and proprietary information to the Company for the limited purpose and under the terms and conditions set forth herein.



NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the cooperation and delivery by [YOURCOMPANY], the Company hereto agrees as follows:



1. Definitions



“CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION” as used herein shall include any and all written and verbal information provided by [YOURCOMPANY] in connection herewith that is not otherwise available from public sources, including but not limited to financial information, customer information, trade secrets, human resources information and other business information.  Additionally, the fact that [YOURCOMPANY] is contemplating a possible acquisition is expressly included as part of the definition of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION.  



The terms “person” and “entity” as used herein shall be broadly interpreted to include the media and any corporation, partnership, group, individual or other entity.  



2. Acknowledgments



The Company acknowledges the representations of [YOURCOMPANY] that CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION is proprietary and valuable to [YOURCOMPANY] and that any disclosure or unauthorized use thereof may cause irreparable harm and loss to [YOURCOMPANY].



The Company acknowledges that neither [YOURCOMPANY] nor any of its representatives make any representation or warranty, express or implied, as to the quality, accuracy, completeness or materiality of any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION.  The Company agrees that neither [YOURCOMPANY] nor any of its representatives shall have any liability to the Company or any of the Company’s representatives relating to or resulting from the use of any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION.  Any and all of the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION furnished by [YOURCOMPANY] is provided as a convenience only and any reliance on or use of same is at the Company’s sole risk.  



The Company acknowledges and agrees that the restrictions and conditions set forth in this Agreement are commercially reasonable in these circumstances.  



3. Obligations of the Company with regard to CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION



In consideration of the disclosure to the Company of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, the Company agrees to receive and to treat CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION on a confidential and restricted basis and to undertake the following additional obligations with respect thereto;



(a) To use CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION for the sole purpose of evaluating the feasibility of an acquisition of [YOURCOMPANY] by the Company.



(b) Not to duplicate, in whole or in part, any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION.



(c) Not to disclose CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to any entity, person, corporation, partnership, sole proprietorship, customer, or client without the prior express written consent of [YOURCOMPANY]; except to those individuals listed below, who have acknowledged this Agreement by their respective signatures.  To the extent the Company intends to disclose CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to advisors who need to know such information for the purpose of assisting the Company in its consideration of the acquisitions, the Company must provide a copy of this Agreement to such advisors and ensure compliance herewith by such advisors.  



(d) To return all CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to [YOURCOMPANY] upon request therefore and to destroy any additional notes or records made from such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION.



The standard of care to be utilized by the Company in the performance of its obligations set forth herein shall be the standard of care utilized by the Company in treating the Company's own information that it does not wish disclosed outside of the Company.



4.  Nondisclosure of Negotiations



The potential for an acquisition of [YOURCOMPANY] is not public information.  In consideration of the disclosure to the Company of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, the Company shall not disclose to any entity, person, corporation, partnership, sole proprietorship, customer, or client without the prior express written consent of [YOURCOMPANY] the fact that CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION has been made available to the Company, that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning a possible acquisition of [YOURCOMPANY] or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect thereto, including the status hereof,  existence of, status of, or potential for any negotiations for the acquisition of [YOURCOMPANY].  



5.  Non-contact with [YOURCOMPANY] Employees, Independent Contractors, or Customers



The Company will not directly or indirectly contact any employees, independent contractors, customers, potential customers, or any other person revealed to the Company as a direct or indirect consequence of the disclosure to the Company of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION without the prior written consent of [YOURCOMPANY].  



6.  Non-Compete with [YOURCOMPANY]



With respect to attachment A, current client and prospect list, in consideration of the disclosure to the Company of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, the Company agrees that for a period of two (2) years following the date of this Agreement, the Company shall not directly, indirectly, on its behalf or on behalf of any other person or entity, contact or solicit, or direct any person or entity to contact or solicit, anyone for the purpose of providing or offering to provide any web design, web development, interactive marketing, or any other service offered by [YOURCOMPANY] as of the date of this Agreement.  Additionally, during this two (2) year period, the Company shall not take any action that is designed to or intended to or may reasonably be expected to have the effect of discouraging any customer, prospective customer, employee or potential employee from entering into or continuing or maintaining a business relationship with [YOURCOMPANY], except for any previous, current or prospect customers that are common to Company disclosure and recipient as identified in attachment A by recipient.



7.  Right to Terminate Negotiations



The Company agrees that unless and until a definitive agreement regarding the acquisition has been executed, [YOURCOMPANY] reserves the right, in its sole discretion, to reject any and all proposals made by the Company and to terminate discussions and negotiations with Company at any time without any liability to the Company.  



8. Remedies



The parties hereto agree that [YOURCOMPANY] would suffer irreparable harm from a breach of any of the covenants or agreements set forth in this Agreement.  In the event of a breach or threatened breach by the Company, [YOURCOMPANY] or its respective successors or assigns may, in addition to all other rights and remedies existing in its favor, apply to any court of competent jurisdiction for specific performance and injunctive or other relief, without any requirements for the securing or posting of any bond in connection with such remedy, in order to enforce or prevent any violations of the provisions hereof.  In the event that [YOURCOMPANY] is required to take action to enforce any of the provisions of this Agreement, [YOURCOMPANY] shall be entitled to reimbursement of all of its costs and expenses incurred therewith, including reasonable attorneys’ fees.  



9.  Survival



The restrictions and obligations of Paragraphs 3, 4, 5 and 6 of this Agreement shall survive any expiration, termination, or cancellation of this Agreement and shall continue to bind the Company, its successors, and assigns.



10.  Assignment



The Company may not assign any of its rights or obligations in this Agreement unless agreed to in writing by [YOURCOMPANY].



11. Amendment or Modification



This Agreement may be amended or modified only by a writing signed by a duly authorized representative of the parties hereto.



12.  Entire agreement



This Agreement contains the entire agreement and understanding between the parties hereto and supersedes any prior or contemporaneous written or oral agreements, representations, and warranties between them respecting the subject matter hereof.  



13. Governing Law and Jurisdiction



This Agreement shall be constructed and enforced in accordance with the laws of Georgia.  The parties agree that any action related to this Agreement shall be venued solely in the courts of Cobb County, Georgia, and the parties irrevocably submit themselves to personal jurisdiction in said courts.  



14.  Severability



The parties agree that each of the provisions included in this Agreement is separate, distinct, and severable from any of the other provisions of this Agreement.  If any provision of this Agreement shall be held invalid or unenforceable under any applicable law, such invalidity or uneforceability shall not affect any other provision of this Agreement that can be given effect without the invalid provision, and, to this end, the provisions hereof are severable.  If any provision of this Agreement is ruled invalid or unenforceable by a court of competent jurisdiction because of a conflict between the provision and any applicable law or public policy, the provision shall be redrawn to make the provision consistent with and valid and enforceable under the law or public policy in question.  





IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the undersigned has caused this Agreement to be executed by its duly authorized representatives.



Signature of all representatives of Company who will have any access to CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION.
